hero.title: AI writes convincingly.
hero.description: 73% of AI-generated texts contain factual errors that look perfectly real. KLAR verifies every single claim against Wikipedia, Google, and the open web — in under 15 seconds. Color-coded Trust Report with source links for every verdict.

problem.title: The Hidden Crisis in AI Content
problem.subtitle: AI doesn't know facts. It predicts words. That's why it invents statistics, fabricates sources, and states wrong dates — all while sounding perfectly confident.
problem.stat1Desc: Studies show AI models regularly hallucinate false facts, citations, and statistics
problem.stat2Desc: KLAR checks up to 50,000 characters with source evidence in real-time
problem.stat3Desc: Fact check, AI detection, bias analysis, plagiarism check, and quality evaluation — simultaneously
problem.painJournalistDesc: Press releases, studies, and statements — every claim checked against Wikipedia and the open web. With source links.
problem.painBusinessDesc: Before an AI-generated report goes to a client: check facts, detect bias, ensure originality.
problem.painStudentDesc: ChatGPT wrote you a text? Check which facts are true and which are completely fabricated.

howItWorks.title: How KLAR Verifies Text
howItWorks.subtitle: A 10-step pipeline. Not one AI guessing — a system proving.
howItWorks.step1Desc: Paste text (up to 50,000 chars), upload PDF/DOCX/TXT up to 10 MB, or enter any article URL.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Statistical NLP detects AI origin, measures writing quality with MECE/Red Team/BLUF/Pre-Mortem frameworks.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Google Gemini Flash identifies every factual statement as structured, verifiable claims.
howItWorks.step4Desc: Each claim is searched against Wikipedia, Wikidata, and Google. 3-5 sources per claim.
howItWorks.step5Desc: Green = supported, red = contradicted, yellow = unverifiable. Every verdict with sources and reasoning.

engines.title: 5 Specialized Analysis Engines
engines.subtitle: Each engine does one thing exceptionally well. Together they give you the complete picture.
engines.factCheckDesc: Extracts individual claims and verifies each against Wikipedia and Google Search. Every verdict includes clickable source links and plain-English reasoning. The core of KLAR.
engines.aiDetectionDesc: Analyzes sentence variety, vocabulary diversity, and predictability patterns using statistical NLP — not AI detecting AI. Instant results, zero cost. Score from 0-100%.
engines.biasDetectionDesc: Identifies loaded language, emotional appeals, one-sided framing, source imbalance, and political lean. Bias score 0-100 with detailed breakdown.
engines.plagiarismDesc: Compares text fingerprints against web sources. Returns originality percentage and a list of matching sources found online.
engines.qualityDesc: Grades your text using four professional frameworks: MECE (structure), Red Team (weaknesses), BLUF (clarity), Pre-Mortem (risks). Overall grade A through F.
engines.fileUploadDesc: Upload PDF, DOCX, or TXT files up to 10 MB. Or enter any article URL — KLAR extracts the content automatically and runs every analysis you select.

features.title: Why 10,000+ Users Choose KLAR
features.evidenceBasedDesc: Every claim is checked against real, external sources — Wikipedia, Wikidata, and Google. Not AI judging AI, but AI verified against fact.
features.transparentDesc: Every verdict comes with a plain-English explanation and clickable source links. You see exactly why each claim was judged supported, contradicted, or unverifiable.
features.universalDesc: Paste text directly, upload PDF/DOCX/TXT files, or enter a URL. Works with AI-generated content, press releases, research papers, blog posts — anything with factual claims.
features.euCompliantDesc: All data stored in Frankfurt, Germany. Row Level Security on every table. No tracking cookies, no data selling. EU AI Act ready with transparent AI decision-making.
features.fastDesc: Real-time streaming shows each claim being verified as it happens. Full Trust Report with all 5 analysis engines typically completes in 10-15 seconds.
features.accessibleDesc: Paste text. Click analyze. Read your report. No coding, no setup, no configuration. Works for journalists, students, researchers, and business professionals alike.

whoIsItFor.title: Who Uses KLAR?
whoIsItFor.subtitle: From individual fact-checkers to enterprise compliance teams
whoIsItFor.journalistDesc: Verify claims in press releases, statements, and articles before publishing. Catch AI-fabricated statistics and false attributions.
whoIsItFor.studentDesc: Check AI-generated study materials for accuracy. Ensure research papers cite real sources. Detect plagiarism.
whoIsItFor.businessDesc: Audit AI-generated reports, proposals, and client documentation before they leave your organization.
whoIsItFor.creator: Content Creators
whoIsItFor.creatorDesc: Ensure blog posts, social media content, and articles are factually accurate before publishing to your audience.
whoIsItFor.legalDesc: Verify contracts, compliance documents, and legal research. Detect bias in AI-drafted documents.
whoIsItFor.educationDesc: Ensure teaching materials are accurate. Check student submissions for AI-generated content and plagiarism.

pricing.subtitle: No credit card required. 3 free checks without even signing up.

trust.subtitle: Your data never leaves the EU. Security is not a feature — it's the foundation.
trust.gdprDesc: Full compliance with EU data protection regulation
trust.euDataDesc: All data stored in Frankfurt, Germany (Supabase EU)
trust.aiActDesc: Transparent AI decision-making with full audit trail
trust.builtInGermanyDesc: Designed, built, and operated from Germany
trust.rlsDesc: Each user can only see their own data — cryptographically enforced
trust.noTrackingDesc: No tracking cookies, no analytics, no data selling to third parties

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: KLAR's accuracy depends on source availability. Claims verifiable against Wikipedia and reliable web sources receive highly accurate verdicts. Very recent events or niche topics may be classified as 'Unverifiable' when no reliable source exists — which is the honest answer.
faq.q2: How is KLAR different from asking ChatGPT to fact-check?
faq.a2: ChatGPT cannot access the internet in real-time and frequently fabricates sources. KLAR actually searches live sources (Wikipedia, Wikidata, Google) and provides clickable links to every source used. Every claim is verified against real, external evidence — not AI opinion.
faq.a3: KLAR stores the extracted claims and their verdicts so you can access your verification history. All data is stored in Frankfurt (EU) with Row Level Security. You can export or delete your data at any time (GDPR Art. 17 & 20).
faq.a4: The interface is available in German and English. The analysis engine processes text in many languages since source search works across languages.
faq.a5: KLAR uses Google Gemini 2.5 Flash in a two-pass architecture: first extracting claims, then evaluating them against live evidence ('Google Search Grounding'). Bias detection, AI detection, and plagiarism checking use statistical NLP — no AI calls needed.
faq.q6: Can I use KLAR for my business?
faq.a6: Yes. The Pro plan ($19/month) is designed for professional use. The Team plan ($49/user/month) adds API access, concurrent analyses, and team dashboards. For Enterprise requirements, contact us.
